summary.applicability_rationale: The web-conference application uses Socket.io (WebSocket-based) for real-time signaling and WebRTC for peer-to-peer media. It does not use the WebTransport API. The Editor's Draft closely mirrors the Official Release with minor editorial differences. The same fundamental gap applies: the application lacks HTTP/3/QUIC support required by WebTransport. The server is Express.js HTTP/1.1.

findings.requirement: WebTransport provides a general-purpose bidirectional communication capability for the web using HTTP/3 as the underlying transport.

evidence.observation: The application uses Node.js http module with Express.js, creating an HTTP/1.1 server. Socket.io provides the real-time bidirectional communication layer over WebSocket. No HTTP/3, QUIC, or WebTransport is used.
evidence.gap_description: WebTransport requires HTTP/3 over QUIC. The application uses HTTP/1.1 with WebSocket.

findings.remediation: To adopt WebTransport, both server and client would need fundamental transport layer changes.

findings.requirement: Conformance requirements use RFC 2119 keywords and apply to user agents implementing the WebTransport API.

evidence.observation: This application is a web application, not a user agent. Conformance to this section is the responsibility of browser implementors.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — conformance requirements are directed at user agents.

findings.requirement: Defines abstract protocol concepts: WebTransport sessions, streams, and datagrams over HTTP/3.

evidence.observation: The application does not use WebTransport protocol concepts. WebRTC and Socket.io fulfill the roles of media transport and signaling, respectively.
evidence.gap_description: No WebTransport protocol concepts are implemented.

findings.remediation: No action required unless migrating to WebTransport.

findings.requirement: A WebTransport session represents a client-server connection over HTTP/3 with states (connecting, connected, closed, failed) and support for datagrams and streams.

evidence.observation: Sessions are established via Socket.io, not WebTransport. Socket.io provides a different session model based on WebSocket with automatic reconnection.
evidence.gap_description: Socket.io sessions differ fundamentally from WebTransport sessions in capabilities (no multiplexed streams, no unreliable datagrams).

findings.remediation: To adopt WebTransport, replace Socket.io with WebTransport constructor and session management.

findings.requirement: WebTransport streams are independent bidirectional or unidirectional byte streams multiplexed over a session with backpressure and flow control.

evidence.observation: Signaling uses Socket.io event-based messaging, not WebTransport streams. All signaling messages (offer, answer, ICE candidates) share a single WebSocket connection.
evidence.gap_description: Socket.io does not provide independent multiplexed streams. A slow or large signaling message can block others on the same connection (head-of-line blocking).

findings.remediation: WebTransport streams could provide independent channels for different signaling types, eliminating head-of-line blocking.

findings.requirement: Defines the interface for sending unreliable datagrams through a WebTransport session.

evidence.observation: No datagram-based communication is used. All signaling uses reliable delivery via Socket.io.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — no datagram interface is used.

findings.requirement: Defines internal slots, attributes, and write algorithms for datagram writable streams.

evidence.observation: No WebTransportDatagramsWritable objects exist in the application.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — no WebTransport API usage.

findings.requirement: Provides duplex datagram stream interface with configurable high/low water marks, maxAge settings, readable and writable datagram streams.

evidence.observation: No datagram duplex streams are used.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — no WebTransport datagrams are used.

findings.requirement: Defines configuration for datagram behavior including maxDatagramSize, incomingMaxAge, outgoingMaxAge, incomingHighWaterMark, outgoingHighWaterMark.

evidence.observation: No datagram configuration is used in the application.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — no WebTransport API usage.

findings.requirement: Main WebTransport interface providing constructor, attributes, methods for sessions, streams, and datagrams.

evidence.observation: Socket.io is used instead of WebTransport for all connection management.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — no WebTransport interface is used.

findings.requirement: WebTransport objects maintain internal slots: [[State]], [[Session]], [[ReadyPromise]], [[ClosedPromise]], [[IncomingBidirectionalStreams]], [[IncomingUnidirectionalStreams]], [[Datagrams]], etc.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport objects are instantiated.

findings.requirement: WebTransport(url, options) constructor validates HTTPS URL, handles certificate hashes, establishes HTTP/3 connection, and creates session.

evidence.observation: Connection is established via Socket.io, not WebTransport constructor.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — WebTransport constructor is not used.

findings.requirement: Defines ready, reliability, congestionControl, closed, draining, datagrams attributes and stream creation methods.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport attributes are accessed.

findings.requirement: Methods include getStats(), close(), createBidirectionalStream(), createUnidirectionalStream(), createSendGroup().

evidence.observation: None of these methods are called.

findings.requirement: Procedures for creating send/receive and bidirectional streams on the session.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport stream creation procedures are executed.

findings.standard_section: 6.6. Session termination not initiated by the client
findings.requirement: Defines server-initiated session termination handling with error handling and promise rejection.

evidence.observation: Session termination is handled through Socket.io disconnect and manual cleanup. WebTransport session termination events are not used.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — no WebTransport session termination handling.

findings.standard_section: 6.7-6.8. Context cleanup steps and Garbage Collection
findings.requirement: Defines cleanup and GC behavior for WebTransport objects.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport objects to clean up.

findings.requirement: WebTransportOptions includes allowPooling, requireUnreliable, serverCertificateHashes, congestionControl.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport configuration options are used.

findings.requirement: Defines dictionaries for close info, send options, and send stream options.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport dictionaries are used.

findings.requirement: Statistics dictionaries for connection and datagram metrics.

evidence.observation: No transport statistics are collected in the application.

findings.requirement: WebTransportSendStream extends WritableStream for sending data with getStats() and sendOrder.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport send streams are used.

findings.standard_section: 7.1-7.6. SendStream Attributes, Methods, Internal Slots, Procedures, Stats
findings.requirement: Complete specification of send stream behavior including write algorithms, abort handling, and statistics.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport send streams exist in the application.

findings.requirement: WebTransportSendGroup for grouping send streams with shared priority.

evidence.observation: No send groups are used.

findings.requirement: WebTransportReceiveStream extends ReadableStream with getStats() method.

evidence.observation: No WebTransport receive streams are used.

findings.requirement: Combines readable and writable into a bidirectional stream.

evidence.observation: No bidirectional WebTransport streams are used.

findings.requirement: Extends WritableStreamDefaultWriter with atomicWrite() for transactional writes.

evidence.observation: No WebTransportWriter is used.

findings.requirement: WebTransportError extends DOMException with source and streamErrorCode attributes.

evidence.observation: No WebTransportError handling exists.

findings.requirement: Maps WebTransport API to HTTP/3 over QUIC protocol features.

evidence.observation: The server uses HTTP/1.1 (http.createServer), not HTTP/3 or QUIC. No protocol mapping to WebTransport exists.
evidence.gap_description: Complete absence of HTTP/3 and QUIC transport support.

findings.remediation: Would require architectural migration to HTTP/3-capable server infrastructure.

findings.requirement: Mandates HTTPS, addresses confidentiality, state persistence, protocol security, certificate hash authentication, and fingerprinting.

source_files.code_snippet:
services:
  - type: web
    name: web-conference
    runtime: node

evidence.observation: HTTPS is provided by Render.com at the edge. The application does not implement WebTransport-specific security considerations.
evidence.gap_description: Not applicable — WebTransport security model is not in effect.

findings.remediation: No action needed. WebTransport security would be inherited if/when adopting the protocol.

findings.requirement: Non-normative examples of WebTransport usage.

evidence.observation: Informative examples only.

findings.remediation: Useful reference material for future WebTransport adoption.

recommendations.title: Evaluate WebTransport for signaling channel (Editor's Draft tracks latest features)
recommendations.description: The Editor's Draft may contain additional features or refinements not yet in the official release. If considering WebTransport adoption, monitor the Editor's Draft for changes to stream priority (send groups), datagram configuration, and connection statistics that could benefit real-time signaling. QUIC 0-RTT could reduce initial connection latency for returning users.

recommendations.title: Consider unreliable datagrams for ICE candidate trickle
recommendations.description: ICE candidates in WebRTC signaling are time-sensitive and can tolerate some loss (later candidates may supersede earlier ones). WebTransport unreliable datagrams could provide lower-latency ICE candidate exchange compared to reliable Socket.io delivery.

recommendations.title: Track Editor's Draft evolution for browser compatibility updates
recommendations.description: The Editor's Draft often leads the official release by including features in active development. Monitoring it helps anticipate upcoming WebTransport capabilities that might make adoption more practical for video conferencing signaling use cases.
